source_name: Code4rena Ethena Findings and Analysis Report (2023-10)
finding_title: Malicious users can front-run to cause a denial of service (DoS) for StakedUSDe due to MinShares checks
summary: Malicious users can transfer USDe token to StakedUSDe protocol directly lead to a denial of service (DoS) for StakedUSDe due to the limit shares check.
root_cause: Report-described logic/accounting flaw: Malicious users can transfer USDe token to StakedUSDe protocol directly lead to a denial of service (DoS) for StakedUSDe due to the limit shares check.
broken_invariants: The withdrawal-flow flow should preserve protocol accounting, authorization, and user fund invariants.
The minting-flow flow should preserve protocol accounting, authorization, and user fund invariants.
The token flow should preserve protocol accounting, authorization, and user fund invariants.
attack_preconditions: the affected code version and configuration match the public report
the report-described function sequence or state condition is reachable
attacker_capability: external user or protocol participant able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: observe accounting, access-control, or availability divergence

impact.description: Malicious users can transfer USDe token to StakedUSDe protocol directly lead to a denial of service (DoS) for StakedUSDe due to the limit shares check.

why_not_higher: the final report classified this as Medium; impact or likelihood is bounded relative to High severity
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with concrete affected code paths and impact narrative
common_false_positive_angles: verify reachability under current roles, pause state, and integration configuration
check whether audited commit and line links match the code under review
check whether sponsor-side operational assumptions fully remove the exploit path
mitigations: We can solve this issue by setting a minimum deposit amount. ### Assessed type DoS
